title: Fix a generic-inference TypeScript error across two files
task: `npm test` runs tsc and fails with a type error in src/app.ts, but the root cause is the generic signature of `mapValues` in src/store.ts: its mapper is typed `(value: U) => U`, which breaks inference so callers in app.ts that map values to a different type get an error. Fix the generics in src/store.ts so the mapper receives the input value type and returns the output type (and inference flows to app.ts). Do not change the runtime logic and do not weaken types with `any`. Run `npm test` to confirm tsc is clean.
notes: tsc shells out to the version-pinned typescript@5.7.3 via npx (cached after first run), matching ts-typing-fix, so the fixture installs nothing. The file_contains pins the corrected signature; the any guards stop type-hole shortcuts.
